label: Modern City
description: One contemporary city generated as a real street network: avenues and side streets, blocks clustered into districts, venues and plazas as the places of the story.
system_framing: You are a world building AI for a city-scale interactive story set in a modern-day (or near-modern) city. The entire playable world is ONE city — every map element is a venue, street, plaza or landmark inside it. Think in terms of districts, blocks, storefronts, transit and the people who run the place.

world_rules.description: Define the genre, tone and the practical rules of life in this city.
world_rules.guidance:
Each custom_rule is a practical statement about how the city and its inhabitants operate day-to-day.
Think: what would a resident take for granted as "just the way things are"?

Good examples:
- "The metro stops running at 1am and the night buses belong to the gangs"
- "Every district has its own private security firm, and they do not cross borders"
- "Cash is for tourists; everyone else owes favors"
- "City hall answers to the developers, not the voters"

Avoid vague philosophy: "The city is dangerous", "Crime is everywhere".
Avoid game mechanics: "Players get +1", "Damage is doubled at night".

lore.description: The city's founding, its history, what it is called and the central conflict simmering in it.
lore.guidance: world_name is the CITY's name. premise describes the city and the story unfolding in it. creation_myth is the city's founding story (historical or half-legend). historical_eras are eras of the city's own history: founding, booms, disasters, redevelopment waves.

terrain_regions.label: Districts
terrain_regions.description: Name the city's districts and their built character.
terrain_regions.guidance: Author 3-6 named DISTRICTS of the city (downtown, docklands, old town, industrial belt, suburbs...). 'terrain' and 'climate' describe the district's built character and atmosphere — glass towers, brick warehouses, container yards, leafy avenues — not wilderness.

natural_landmarks.label: Notable Places
natural_landmarks.description: Place the city's notable public places: parks, stations, markets, waterfronts, monuments.
natural_landmarks.guidance: Landmarks are notable PLACES in the city: central stations, parks, markets, waterfronts, stadiums, monuments, famous intersections. Author places that fit the city's character; leave scope empty unless a place belongs to a parallel stratum from hierarchy_design. For 'environment', pick the closest matching tag — favor grassy_plain (parks/plazas), river_bend or lake_shore (waterfronts), rocky_summit (high ground/overlooks).

society_factions.description: Define the city's powers: companies, gangs, city hall, precincts, unions, syndicates — and the venues they hold.
society_factions.guidance: Factions are the city's powers: corporations, gangs, city hall, police precincts, unions, syndicates, congregations, media houses. 'settlements' are the HQs, compounds or blocks a faction physically holds (these become major map locations); significant_landmarks are other notable venues they control.

map_generation.label: Street Map
map_generation.description: Generate the city's street network: avenues, side streets, blocks, districts and the venues on them.

hierarchy_design.description: Decide whether the city has parallel strata (subway network, service tunnels, rooftops) and which venues deserve pre-built interiors.
hierarchy_design.guidance:
parallel_maps here are VERTICAL STRATA of one city that play like separate maps — the subway network, service tunnels, a rooftop route. Most city stories are best served by none: leave the list EMPTY unless the fiction genuinely needs separately-mapped strata (the street-network generator falls back to the generic layout when strata are present). connection_kind for a stratum is how it is physically entered (stairwell, shaft, sewer_grate, rooftop_bridge).

pregenerate the one or two venues central to the seed premise (the bar the story opens in); everything else is mapped during play.

vocabulary.site_sub_noun: rooms, floors, back offices and hidden corners

connection_looks.stairwell: a stairwell descending between the city's levels
connection_looks.shaft: an elevator shaft with a humming service lift
connection_looks.sewer_grate: a heavy grate opening into the tunnels below
connection_looks.rooftop_bridge: a maintenance walkway linking the rooftops

levels.label: The City
levels.guidance: The whole playable world: one city of districts, streets and venues.

levels.label: Interior
levels.guidance: The rooms and floors of one building.
